meaning: n.记日记者,负日记专责的人,日记作者
examples: Walking home after dinner, your diarist gets hopelessly lost.
Your diarist has (by British standards) excellent German. But it is hard to use it.
Your diarist wrote condolence notes to every Pole in his contacts book after the tragedy.
As secretary to his father in Europe, he became an accomplished linguist and assiduous diarist.
In 1942, the diarist Anne Frank and her family took refuge from the Nazis in Amsterdam.
